stmt: There exist Γ, Δ, Λ, A, B such that LeftRep Γ A and RightRep (Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ) B, while Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ ⇒ B but neither LeftRep (Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ) B nor RightRep (Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ) B.
summary_delta: The new theorem shows that same-orientation composition is closed. A mixed-orientation escape theorem would give the sharp failure-of-converse boundary now visible: substitution preserves one-sidedness only within a fixed orientation, which materially strengthens the fragment taxonomy rather than merely adding another local counterexample.
priority_rationale: A mixed-orientation escape theorem would sharpen the boundary of same-orientation closure in a reusable way.

stmt: LeftRep is the least relation R on sequents such that R [A] A holds for every type A, R is closed under the left-fragment slash rules, and whenever R Γ A and R (Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ) B then R (Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ) B.
summary_delta: The solved theorem upgrades LeftRep from an external recognizer to a cut-stable structural fragment. A least-closure characterization would make that fragment intrinsic and rule-theoretic, giving an exact interface theorem in the style of structural reducibility and subsystem classification emphasized by the agenda and materials.
priority_rationale: A least-closure characterization would make LeftRep intrinsic rather than recognizer-like.

stmt: Let `prove1_right` be the variant of `prove1` obtained by deleting all `.ldiv` branches from the atomic `candidates` search. Then ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), prove1_right Γ A = true ↔ ∃ n : Nat, RightFocusedTree n Γ A
summary_delta: The proof logs exposed a natural forgetful bridge from right-focused trees to ordinary focused trees; the next substantive step is to turn that bridge into an exact proof-search interface. This would connect homogeneous proof objects, candidate decomposition, and fragment membership in the precise way the current frontier is missing, and it would yield a genuinely intrinsic right-fragment decision procedure rather than another recognizer reformulation.
priority_rationale: This gives an exact right-side search interface linking prove1, homogeneous witnesses, and membership.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (s : String), LeftRep Γ (Tp.atom s) ↔ (Γ = [Tp.atom s] ∨ ∃ c ∈ candidates Γ, match c with | Cand.ldiv Pi Δ A B Λ => LeftRep Δ A ∧ LeftRep (Pi ++ [B] ++ Λ) (Tp.atom s) | _ => False)
summary_delta: The solved theorem was obtained by excluding the ambient `rdiv` branch in atomic sequent decomposition using left-onlyness. Packaging that exclusion as an exact candidate-level characterization would create the missing bridge from homogeneous candidate search to fragment membership, which the theory state explicitly identifies as a frontier gap and which is stronger than another local decomposition lemma.
priority_rationale: An exact left atomic candidate criterion fills a named search-to-fragment gap on the pressured side.

stmt: For every context Γ and atom s, RightRep Γ (# s) iff either Γ = [# s] or there exists a right-division candidate `Cand.rdiv pi B A delta lam` in `candidates Γ` such that RightRep delta A and RightRep (pi ++ [B] ++ lam) (# s).
summary_delta: This packages the new recursive right-fragment reduction in the repository’s actual candidate-search language, giving the missing exact bridge from homogeneous candidate shape to fragment membership rather than only an existential decomposition.
priority_rationale: Exact right atomic candidate syntax would be reusable search packaging, though less urgent than the left analogue.

stmt: Give the mirror-image exact classification for the left-only fragment: for every context Γ and atom s, Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s iff there exist n >= 0, types A_1, ..., A_n, and blocks Δ_1, ..., Δ_n such that Γ = Δ_n ++ ... ++ Δ_1 ++ [A_1 ⧹ (A_2 ⧹ ... (A_n ⧹ Tp.atom s) ...)] and each Δ_i ⇒ A_i.
summary_delta: This would isolate the left-handed analogue of the right-fragment head normal form, showing that the one-step atomic decomposition scales to a sharp structural criterion rather than staying a local proof-script fact. It also advances the fragment taxonomy inside the current theory instead of adding another nearby slash lemma.
priority_rationale: A left exact head-normal form would materially extend the fragment taxonomy, but only at atomic conclusions.

stmt: Let `prove1_left` be the variant of `prove1` obtained by deleting all `.rdiv` branches from the atomic `candidates` search and rejecting `Tp.rdiv` goals. Then for all `Γ : List Tp` and `A : Tp`, `prove1_left Γ A = true ↔ LeftRep Γ A`.
summary_delta: The solved result turns `LeftRep` into an intrinsic left-calculus notion, so the next high-value step is to make one-sided membership algorithmically exact rather than recognizer-like. This directly addresses the current bottleneck around left search bridges and is strictly stronger than an atomic-only candidate characterization.

stmt: For all `Γ : List Tp` and `A : Tp`, if every formula in `A :: Γ` is in the image of `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.toProductFree`, then `LeftRep Γ A ↔ Γ ⇒ A`.
summary_delta: This would sharpen the new translation theorem into an ambient boundary statement: on genuinely left-only sequents, ordinary derivability coincides exactly with left-fragment membership. That is a reusable subsystem classification result, not just a repackaging of the witness existence theorem.

stmt: Whenever `Γ ⇒ A` and `Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ ⇒ B` admit left-homogeneous focused witnesses, `Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ ⇒ B` also admits a left-homogeneous focused witness.
summary_delta: The theory already has same-orientation cut closure at the derivability level, and the current frontier explicitly lacks witness-level substitution closure. Extending closure from existential fragment predicates to compositional focused proof objects would connect subsystem membership, proof objects, and normalization-style structure in one theorem.
